export default "---\ntitle: God is an Introvert\ndate: 2022-02-25\nslug: god-is-an-introvert\ntags: [philosophy, opinion]\nsummary: A personal, speculative take on what kind of personality God might have, assuming He/She exists.\n---\n\n> *I have expressed my **personal views** in this blog and do not intend to propagate any religious beliefs through this.*\n\nContrary to what the title may suggest, **I am an atheist**.\n\nThis is just going to be my views on **what God may be** and more specifically a **discussion on His/Her personality type**.\n\n### How God Works\n\nIf such a being exists, **why does He/She not exhibit His/Her existence**? Why are a lot of intelligent beings of the opinion that **He/She doesn't exist**?\n\nThe answer to this may be, assuming He/She exists, that **He/She is just an introvert** who likes to leave the world as be and **only interfere when required**. This also makes sense considering, most of the activities recorded and preserved in the modern digital age are sewed, and the outcomes are **reaped by humans and their products**.\n\nAlmost all the major strategic inflection points seen in tech that I can recall of since the early 2000s — from the **iPod in 2001**, the **APIs in 2003**, **Facebook in 2004**, **Youtube in 2005**, the **iPhone in 2007**, the **iPad in 2010**, **VRs in late 2010s**, etc. — apart from the more regular wars, political conflicts, etc., were **made by and influenced humans**.\n\nIf God were an extrovert, He/She would have communicated with the greatest leaders of this era like **Steve Jobs, Mark Zuckerberg, Elon Musk, or Linus Torvalds** and helped them change the world a bit more and a bit faster.\n\n### Beliefs of humans\n\nI have always wondered **why the most faithful of all humans have backup plans**. Isn't God going to ensure their plans get executed and they were foresighted? If not, why have the faith anyway? I am not encouraging anyone to lose faith here, all I am doing is trying to find out **why and how, if at all, God exists**.\n\nMy realization of this problem is that God is not just an introvert, **He/She is probably ignorant of petty issues** that mortals face anyway. It may not be **computationally feasible** for Him/Her at this point with the **human population at nearly 8 billion**. Or it may just happen that the solutions mortals arrive at whilst interacting with each other pleases God to an extent that He/She doesn't bother modifying the relations among them or the outcome they conclude. Either way, the perspective He/She leaves mortals to ponder is that **God might be an introvert**.\n\n### Another perspective\n\nA close friend/senior suggested I start watching the series **Lucifer on Netflix**, and it helped me **see the world differently**. One important realization I had from it is that **God *(and Devil)* may exist among us** and interact with us on a **fairly everyday basis** and yet leave us unaware (to not freak us out).\n\nIn case the human form of God is an introvert **the world would not be much different than it is right now**. Therefore the assumption **'if God ever visits Earth and decides to stay with us, He/She would act like an introvert'** may not be very far-fetched.\n"
